title: A Two-Stage CNN-TTS Framework for Sign Language to Emotion Aware Speech Translation
authors: Thimothi Shibu, Keerthana T.D., Anjana T.P., Athira M.V., Rajeev Rajan
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Bridging the communication gap between hearingimpaired individuals and non-signers remains a persistent challenge. This work presents a software-based framework that converts hand gestures into emotion-aware speech using a twostage pipeline. In the first stage, a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) classifies sign language gestures into corresponding alphabets. Image preprocessing techniques are applied to improve robustness under varying conditions. In the second stage, the generated text is transformed into speech while incorporating emotional context inferred from the text itself. Edge Text-toSpeech (Edge TTS) is used for neural voice generation, and Sound eXchange (SoX) is employed to adjust pitch, tempo, and intensity to reflect emotional tone. The integrated system produces intelligible and expressive speech output, offering a practical assistive communication solution with potential for future expansion into continuous sentence translation and multilingual support.

title: Olaf: Bringing an Animated Character to Life in the Physical World_supp1-3685938.mp4
authors: David Mueller
abstract: Animated characters often move in non-physical ways and have proportions that are far from a typical walking robot. This provides an ideal platform for innovation in both mechanical design and stylized motion control. In this paper, we bring Olaf to life in the physical world, relying on reinforcement learning guided by animation references for control. To create the illusion of Olaf's feet moving along his body, we hide two asymmetric legs under a soft foam skirt. To fit actuators inside the character, we use spherical and planar linkages in the arms, mouth, and eyes. Because the walk cycle results in harsh contact sounds, we introduce additional rewards that noticeably reduce impact noise. The large head, driven by small actuators in the character's slim neck, creates a risk of overheating, amplified by the costume. To keep actuators from overheating, we feed temperature values as additional inputs to policies, introducing new rewards to keep them within bounds. We validate the efficacy of our modeling in simulation and through experiments on the robot.

title: Multi-Connectivity for Industrial Automation: Achieving Deterministic Reliability through Private 5G, Public 5G/LTE, and Wi-Fi Integration
authors: William O’Brien, Adam Dooley, Mihai Penica, Sean McGrath, Eoin O’Connell
abstract: The transition toward fully wireless industrial automation under Industry 4.0 requires communication systems capable of deterministic and ultra-reliable performance. This study experimentally evaluates the potential of multi-connectivity, specifically the simultaneous use of private 5G, public 5G/LTE, and Wi-Fi in order to meet the 99.9% reliability target defined in 3GPP TS 22.104 for mission-critical applications. Using a mobile robotic platform within the Confirm Smart Manufacturing Centre, four experimental scenarios were conducted under stationary and mobile conditions with 64 byte and 25000 byte payloads. Results show that no individual interface achieved the 99.9% reliability threshold independently, however composite multi-path configurations employing packet duplication across interfaces consistently met or exceeded this benchmark, achieving up to 100% reliability. Dual private 5G + Wi-Fi and triple-interface configurations eliminated single-path deficiencies, while private 5G demonstrated superior stability under mobility due to its single cell architecture. Although packet duplication increases overall network load, it ensures mission-critical communication with a minimum reliability of 99.98%, fulfilling deterministic performance requirements for industrial control and robotic systems. These findings confirm that multi-connectivity offers a practical and robust approach to achieving ultra-reliable wireless communication in industrial environments.

title: SUM4SOFT: A process model to build and update software samples
authors: Juan Andrés Carruthers, Andrea Lezcano Airaldi, Jorge Andrés Diaz-Pace, Emanuel Irrazábal
abstract: The validity of research findings critically depends on the selection of software samples. However, Mining Software Repositories studies often rely on outdated datasets and lack systematic, transparent procedures for repository selection and dataset construction, which hinder their replicability and undermine their findings. In this article, we propose SUM4SOFT, a process model to provide a structured, transparent, and replicable approach for building and maintaining software samples in Mining Software Repositories studies. We designed SUM4SOFT following a four-step methodology based on established sampling protocols from the literature. We validated the model in two empirical studies: (i) a Mining Software Repositories study and (ii) a replication case study conducted with external researchers to test both its effectiveness and clarity. The mining study showed that datasets collected and updated with the model produced consistent distributions across key code metrics. Furthermore, the case study confirmed these findings and provided feedback that led to refinements in the model’s roles, tasks, and work products. Our validation studies demonstrated the effectiveness and practical utility of SUM4SOFT, although further studies in other contexts are needed to generalize its applicability.

title: Towards Well-Connected Retina Segmentation: A Fully Differentiable Endpoint Connectivity Loss (DECL)
authors: Jannik Sobisch, Jaden Myers, Mirco Fuchs, Žiga Bizjak
abstract: Despite achieving high volumetric overlap, pixel-based deep learning segmentation approaches do not ensure true vascular connectivity, leading to fragmentations structurally analogous to Branch Retinal Artery Occlusions. This fundamental deficiency means that even segmentations achieving high pixel-wise overlap metrics (such as the Dice Score) do not guarantee or improve true vascular connectivity, rendering them unreliable for quantitative analysis and subsequent clinical risk stratification of high-risk systemic events, such as stroke. To resolve this, we introduce the Differentiable Endpoint Connectivity Loss (DECL), a novel, fully differentiable loss function that directly optimizes vascular continuity by precisely targeting errors in vessel endpoints. DECL leverages a differentiable soft-skeletonization module to impose two specialized penalties: a Normalized Count Penalty (L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">c</sub>), which rigorously penalizes the discrete difference in the number of predicted versus true endpoints (ΔN<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">p</sub>), and a Normalized Distance Loss (L<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">d</sub>), which penalizes the Euclidean distance between the weighted centers of the true and predicted endpoints. In comparative experiments across four datasets (DRIVE, CHASE DB1, STARE, and OCTA-500), we tested DECL against established methods including Dice CE, clDice, cbDice, and SAC Loss. DECL and its hybrid variant, DECL<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">clDice</sub>, consistently recorded the highest mean Dice Score and clDice scores, specifically, DECL<sub xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">clDice</sub>d improved the mean clDice score from 0.839 to 0.851 on CHASE DB1 and from 0.836 to 0.842 on DRIVE, while minimizing Betti Number Error (β-Error) andVariation of Information. The DECL method establishes a robust, mathematically justified framework for generating high-fidelity vascular maps, essential for accurate biomarker quantification and reliable clinical assessment.

title: Real-Time Dual-Arm Cooperative Manipulation Under Multiple Constraints: A Two-Stage Sampling MPC Approach_supp1-3686224.mp4
authors: Jun Yang
abstract: This paper introduces a novel framework for reactive control in dual-arm cooperative robotic systems, addressing the significant challenges posed by high-dimensional, non-convex optimization demands, intricate kinematic, multi-modal distribution, the need for precise, and synchronized coordination. The core of our approach is a two-stage sampling-based model predictive control, which integrates k-means, dual quaternion, and null space into a cohesive system. This integration enhances the system's ability to manage complex coordination tasks, such as obstacle avoidance and holding a water cup, while mitigating risks associated with local optima and reducing control jitter. Our framework not only improves performance and reliability, but also overcomes the traditional computational bottlenecks inherent in dual-arm coordination. These advancements are validated through extensive simulations and experiments, demonstrating the robustness and efficiency of our proposed methodology.

title: A Fine-Tuned Fuzzy-Optimized UNet++ for Retinal Vessel Segmentation
authors: Kumar JanardanPatra, Jibitesh Mishra, Sanjit Kumar Dash, Dipak Kumar Nidhi, Sudhir Kumar Mohapatra, Jukka Heikkonen, Rajeev Kanth
abstract: The human eye is a vital organ responsible for vision, and the health of the retina is crucial for preserving sight. Retinal vessel segmentation plays a key role in the early detection of ophthalmic diseases such as diabetic retinopathy, glaucoma, and hypertension, where vascular abnormalities indicate disease progression. This study introduces a novel hybrid framework that enhances vessel segmentation performance using Fuzzy-Optimized UNet++ architecture, demonstrating a 2% improvement over the baseline UNet++ (95.3% accuracy). The proposed method is trained and validated on five benchmark datasets DRIVE, HRF, IOSTAR, ARIA, and CHASE_DB1offering diversity in image resolution, pathology, and vessel morphology to evaluate cross-dataset generalization potential. To address limitations such as class imbalance, noise sensitivity, and poor micro-vessel continuity, we incorporate fuzzy logic for enhanced boundary refinement and Harris Hawks Optimization (HHO) for robust parameter tuning and convergence acceleration. Additionally, a synthetic vessel generation module, VesselGAN, is used to expand dataset diversity, achieving an SSIM score of 0.89 while preserving anatomical accuracy. Comprehensive evaluation is performed using 10-fold cross-validation and external testing on five independent datasets RETA, IDRiD, IOSTAR (external), Kaggle, and clinical-grade images. The integrated approach achieves superior performance across all key metrics, including Dice Coefficient, IoU, SSIM, and F1-score, especially under noisy, low-contrast, and ultra-thin vessel conditions. This research presents a unified, end-to-end system that advances the state-of-the-art in retinal vessel segmentation. Its superior accuracy, resilience to data variability indicates robustness across unseen clinical domains and suggest suitability for real-world deployment in ophthalmic diagnostic systems.

title: DA-TSK-PLR-FS: Domain Adaptive Takagi–Sugeno–Kang Fuzzy System via Pseudolabel Refinement for CCTA-Based Vulnerable Coronary Plaques Recognition
authors: Yuanpeng Zhang, Wei Zhang, Xinyun Zhang, Zhaoheng Huang, Sheng Huang, Saikit Lam, Ge Ren, Shitong Wang, Jing Cai
abstract: Artificial intelligence has shown great promise in noninvasive recognition of vulnerable coronary plaques. However, practical data issues in multicenter studies, such as inconsistent data distribution and insufficient or missing data labels, could significantly affect the recognition accuracy. Unsupervised domain adaptation (UDA) can be introduced to address this challenge, but several limits still remain. First, many existing UDA models are black boxes, hindering healthcare professionals' ability to interpret and trust the model's decision. Second, some methods use pseudolabel to enhance performance, but often overlook the quality assessment of these pseudolabels, potentially leading to negative knowledge transfer. To this end, based on the interpretable Takagi–Sugeno–Kang fuzzy system (TSK-FS), a novel domain adaptive method is proposed to improve model generalizability for vulnerable coronary plaques recognition in multicenter data. First of all, TSK-FS is employed to construct a shared fuzzy feature space for the source domain and the target domain, aiming to better align data distribution. To make full use of the information of unlabeled target domain data and further reduce the negative knowledge transfer, the enhanced pseudolabel learning mechanism is further introduced by combining the graph-based random walking and label filtering. Moreover, Multicenter data of 910 patients with suspected or diagnosed coronary artery disease were collected from three hospitals for experiments. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed DA-TSK-PLR-FS achieves the promising generalizability across multicenter datasets

title: MASH: Mask Shuffling-Based Federated Learning for both Cross-Silo and Cross-Device Environments_supp1-3683638.pdf
authors: Jiheng Zhang
abstract: Federated Learning (FL) has been extensively studied over the past few years. Cross-silo FL typically involves tens of reliable organizations with larger-scale, high-quality data sources, while cross-device FL may include thousands of devices, each with limited computation and communication capabilities. However, existing research has yet to simultaneously address cross-silo and cross-device scenarios. We propose a novel mask-shuffling scheme that protects clients' gradient privacy and ensures all masks added to gradients are ultimately canceled out. While gradients are treated as exponentiation values, we eliminate the need to solve the discrete logarithm problem by leveraging a precomputed lookup table. Additionally, we partition all clients into small groups, each performing the mask-shuffling scheme to accelerate mask generation. This partitioning strategy enhances the framework's resilience to client dropouts. To resist forgery attacks on aggregated gradients, each client publishes a commitment to enable verification of its contribution's correctness. We further propose a batch verification method for d-dimensional gradients, reducing the complexity from O(d) to O(1). Besides, the blockchain smart contract serves as a verifier, ensuring public trustworthiness. We formally analyze all the required security properties and conduct a comprehensive comparison with related works. Furthermore, we perform systematic experiments to evaluate overall performance, considering both cross-silo and cross-device environments. The results demonstrate reasonable efficiency, even for models with 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">5</sup>-dimensional gradients across 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">4</sup> devices and 10<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">10</sup>-dimensional gradients across 10 organizations.

title: Leveraging Transformer-Based Language Models to Uncover Stylistic Evolution across Centuries of English Literary Corporate
authors: Jerin Austin Dhas. J, Jithin Jenner E G, M. Vinoth Kumar, Subash Chinnappanathan P, Praveen Kumar. M, V. R. Yasu Bharathi
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The research addresses the question of how the language models of English texts have changed in terms of style in multiple centuries using a transformer-based language model. This will be focused on letter standardization and metadata enrichment of past literary sources, the entire methodological approach in offering the linguistic consistency process using the text classification, and contextualization. The BERT and other model transformer-based approaches can be utilized in extracting high dimensional features that extract the subtle syntactic and semantic models that involve stylistic variations. These features extracted along with metadata-enhancement in the form of authorship, publication year and genre will offer a chance to perform a super analysis of stylistic evolution with time. A Random Forest classifier is applied to classify and predict stylistic features, and the reliable interpretation is included; the features of high stylistic change are singled out. They are proved to be correlated strongly by means of temporal metadata and changing stylistic elements experimentally, which introduces a way of examining the changing (evolving) style era per era. This contribution to the digital literary studies achieves this by the creation of effective pipeline of temporal stylistic analysis and by the highlighting of the usefulness of contemporary, humanities-focused NLP tools in modern research.

title: Testing of Passive Memristive Crossbars in AI Hardware Accelerators
authors: Shanmukha Mangadahalli Siddaramu, Mahta Mayahinia, Surendra Hemaram, Sule Ozev, Mehdi B. Tahoori
abstract: Memristor-based computation-in-memory (CiM) architectures address the growing computational demands of AI accelerators by enabling analog matrix-vector multiplication (MVM) directly within the memory array. Passive (selectorless) memristive crossbars are particularly attractive for such architectures due to their high density and compatibility with back-end-of-line fabrication. Here, AI model parameters are stored as memristor conductances, and MVM is performed in situ by activating multiple rows simultaneously and sensing the resulting column currents. As a result, column currents become the primary observable, making column-level fault detection more relevant for AI workloads than conventional March-based cell-level tests, which are specially time - and energy-intensive for passive crossbars due to specialized biasing methods. To address this, we propose a current-based testing methodology tailored to passive crossbars that detects and diagnoses faulty columns while accounting for non-idealities such as sneak-path currents, line resistance, and process variations. Faulty columns are detected by programming all cells to a uniform state and measuring column-current deviations, followed by targeted test patterns to estimate per-column fault density. On a $64 \times 64$ array, the proposed approach achieves 100% faulty column detection, 98% overall fault coverage, and a $2.3 \times$ speed-up in test time compared to conventional March tests. Hence, providing a fast and efficient solution for manufacturing screening of passive crossbars with sufficient diagnostic resolution to support systemlevel fault tolerance for AI applications.

title: An Efficient Infravision Thermal-Infrared Human Detection System for Low-Visibility Surveillance
authors: Kailash Pantha, Biman Rimal, Kalyan Kumar Shrestha, David Chieng, Jeevan Koiri, Ashma Yonghang, Aniket Kumar Gupt, Anamol Khadka
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Current detection systems struggle to provide real-time, accurate, and location-aware human detection in environments where illumination is poor or visibility is obstructed. Thermal imaging technology has become one of the prominent imaging technologies to capture and visualize the thermal radiation emitted from the body of living beings irrespective of visibility conditions. This work discusses the design and implementation of a low-cost, small and portable thermal imaging based human detection system to assist in low visibility environments such as wildlife conservation areas, harsh search and rescue zones, etc. The system integrates Raspberry Pi 4B with FLIR Lepton 3.5 thermal camera and Neo-6M GPS module, powered by YOLOV3 model to perform human detection on captured images. Our newly trained YOLOv3 model demonstrated good performance, achieving an Average Precision at IoU of 0.5 (AP50) of 0.9404 and a recall of 0.6974. This small and portable system can be easily mounted on a UAV or UGV to support a wide range of crucial and challenging surveillance tasks such as anti-poaching. A Flutter-based mobile application is also developed for real time alert notification with GPS coordinates and images captured, enabling rapid and informed decision making. Hence, the proposed holistic approach of efficient infravision human detection based on thermal imaging significantly enhances security and is directly applicable to crucial domains including anti-poaching, search and rescue and disaster management.

title: BioVisionNet: Deep Morphological Learning for Biomedical Image Segmentation
authors: S. Siva Shankar, Gayathri Parasa, K Kiran, Praveen Mittal, Minu Balakrishnan, Veeraswamy Ammisetty, Rasmi A
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: Biomedical image segmentation is an important task in medical diagnosis, treatment planning and disease monitoring and current deep learning models are often limited by their inability to handle structural distortions as well as their weak boundary delineation and low robustness in varying imaging conditions. To overcome the difficulties, this study proposes a deep morphological learning framework for biomedical segmentation named BioVisionNet to improve the shape preservation and boundary accuracy of the segmentation results. The goal is to combine the learnable morphological operator, multi-scale feature encoding and attention-based decoding in order to overcome the limitations of traditional CNN and transformer models. The proposed methodology takes into account a Morphological Feature Encoding Layer, multi-path BioVision encoder and boundary refining attention decoder that is trained with a structural consistency loss function. Experiments are performed on BRATS 2023 dataset and it achieves a better performance with Dice Score of 0.953, IoU of 0.912, and Hausdorff Distance of 4.8 which is better than 5 stateof-the-art methods. The findings attest to the fact that BioVisionNet brings much better structural fidelity, segmentation reliability and clinical applicability. In conclusion, in this work, a solid basis for a morphology-aided segmentation has been built and holds great promise to be applied to actual medical imaging workflows in the future.

title: Emerging Research Trends in Machine Learning and Deep Learning for Secure and Scalable Cloud Ecosystem
authors: Swetha Sistla
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Cloud-native Machine Learning (ML) and Deep Learning (DL) systems provide the foundations for efficient and flexible intelligence; however, they raise new challenges in terms of privacy, robustness, and multi-tenant governance. We propose a Zero-Trust MLOps with Confidential Execution (ZT-ML-CE) framework that leverages Differential Privacy (DP), Federated Learning (FL), and Trusted Execution Environments (TEE) to protect the training and inference of models in distributed clouds. Experiments on an eight-node hybrid cluster of confidential VMs with NVIDIA A100 GPUs show that with batch size = 128, λ-DDP-SGD with ε = 4.2 reduces membership-inference success rate by 31% for δ = 0.05, while maintaining test accuracy of 97.6% under adversarial training using FGSM (ε<inf xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">adv</inf> = 0.01). TEE-based inference introduces an average latency overhead of 12% while maintaining model integrity and tamper resistance. Compute consumption is reduced by 14% through energy-aware orchestration mechanisms. Comparative evaluation across baseline, DP-only, TEE-only, and full ZT-ML configurations further shows that the framework maintains an effective balance among accuracy, robustness, and scalability. The findings indicate that integrating privacy-preserving learning with confidential computing enables the construction of trustworthy ML pipelines that remain compliant with legal and ethical constraints in multi-cloud ecosystems. This combined approach advances the practical deployment of secure, reliable, and verifiable AI systems at scale, supporting broader adoption of transparent and integrity-driven machine-learning workflows.

title: Conditional-WGAN-GP For Balanced Tabular Data Generation with Explainability
authors: Vaibhav M S, Tanmay M S, Ayush R, Tanmay Tanmay, Ashwini Kodipalli, Merin Thomas
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Class imbalance remains to be a major issue in structured tabular datasets, often leading to biased models that do not learn important aspects of minority class. In this work, we propose a Conditional Wasserstein Generative Adversarial Network with Gradient Penalty (WGAN-GP), that uses attention mechanism for balanced and explainable tabular data generation. The Attention based generator retains the statistical properties of the original dataset while capturing complicated feature interactions. To provide transparency, we analyze both global and local feature contributions of the generator and discriminator using Explainable AI (XAI) techniques such as SHAP and LIME. Our experiments, conducted on multiple tabular datasets including classification and regression tasks, demonstrate that the proposed model generates balanced, high-fidelity synthetic data that closely resembles real samples. The explainability studies further confirm that the model relies on meaningful patterns and semantically related feature relationships, rather than artifacts or noise. Together, these results provide a strong and trustworthy framework that integrates interpretability into generative modelling, making more reliable AI systems and improvements in reducing class imbalance.

title: Hyperparameter Tuning of Deep Learning Models for Smart Water Management Systems
authors: Medhavi Malik, Adel Subhe Abedalkader Abraheem, Mohammed Mahdi Mohammed, Hussein Basim Furaijl, Doaa Saadi Kareem, Ahmad Fadhil
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: The increasing demand for sustainable and efficient water usage has led to the emergence of Smart Water Management Systems (SWMS), which leverage Internet of Things (IoT) sensors and machine learning techniques for real-time monitoring and control. Deep learning models, with their capacity to learn complex patterns from large datasets, have shown great potential in forecasting water demand, detecting leaks, and optimizing resource allocation. However, their performance heavily depends on the proper configuration of hyperparameters such as learning rate, number of layers, batch size, and activation functions. In this paper we focus on the fine-tuning of hyperparameters of deep learning architectures such as LSTM, CNN, and more complex hybrid architectures for better performance and reliability over SWMS applications.

title: Interference Mitigation and Visualization by PSO Optimized Multi-chirp Waveform and Selective Spectral Filtering in a Photonic Radar System
authors: S M Rezwanul Islam, Sun Zhejie, Mohd Rashidi Che Beson, Pankaj Raj Dawadi, Shilong Pan, Bikash Nakarmi
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: This paper proposes and demonstrates an interference-resilient photonic radar system by manipulating Particle Swarm Optimization (PSO) algorithm and introducing a Selective Spectral Filtering (SSF) technique. The PSO algorithm contributes to generate optimized multi-chirp linear frequency modulated (O-MCLFM) waveforms and the SSF technique separates target echo signals from corrupted received signal. While conventional radar waveforms severely suffer from coherent interference, the proposed model produces O-MCLFM to remove ghost targets from the range profile. Then, the SSF process separates target echo signals from corrupted received signal to enhance the peak-to-sidelobe level (PSL) in the range profile. After separation, the interference is constructed for visualization. Simulation results show that the proposed method achieves a PSL of 14.43 dB that closely matches the interference-free performance, with a linewidth of 2 cm and detection error of 1 mm.

title: Bespoke Ad Targeting via LLM-Generated User Profiles and RAG-Based Recommendations
authors: Arpita Vasant Shah, Sargam Menghani, Shuxian Yu, Patrick Jordan
publish: 2026 International Conference on AI x Data and Knowledge Engineering (AIxDKE)
abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have recently demonstrated strong capabilities in analyzing and summarizing user intent from large-scale behavioral data. Ad recommendation systems commonly rely on user interaction data but typically compress it into sparse embeddings, limiting their ability to capture nuanced preferences. We address this gap by leveraging LLMs to analyze user histories as rich, contextual narra-tives-enabling more accurate and explainable recommendations from the same underlying data. In this work, we present the Bespoke Algorithm, an initial experimental study that leverages this foundational capability of LLMs to deliver personalized ad recommendations. First, we summarize complex historical user interaction data into rich, verbose user profiles using LLMs. To ensure transparency, all features extracted for the user profiles are accompanied by supporting context grounded in the specific user interactions from which they were derived, as identified by the LLM through prompts. Next, for retrieval of personalized ad candidates, the model is prompted to produce relevant and complementary search engine optimization (SEO) keywords from past user interactions to promote diversity. These keywords are then used to retrieve ad candidates from our ad corpus via approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) search, effectively grounding the selection. Finally, we employ a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) module that uses the generated user profiles and retrieved ad candidates to classify them into three cate-gories-Recommended, Related, and Not Recommended. Each classification includes a rationale and a confidence score to support downstream decision-making. Experimental results show that the Bespoke Algorithm achieves a 68.6% relative improvement in View-through Click-Through Rate (ViewCTR) compared to a state-of-the-art (SOTA) user embedding model currently deployed in production. These findings highlight the practical value of LLMs in delivering highly personalized advertising solutions.

title: Designing a PPG sensor-based real-time Blood Pressure measurement system
authors: Anum Afroz, Talha Anwar, Ikechi A. Ukaegbu
publish: 2026 International Conference on ICT and Photonics (ICTP)
abstract: High blood pressure is the leading cause of death and disability around the world. It also increases the risk of many serious diseases, such as heart disease and kidney disease. In evaluating and diagnosing cardiovascular diseases, Blood Pressure plays a crucial role in determining stroke and myocardial infractions, which is why it is necessary to monitor a continuous health evaluation of blood pressure. Current methods used to estimate patient blood pressure involve a cuff that may be irritating to some patients with stiff veins. So, to save time and prevent the pain of patients that is caused by inflation and deflation at a continuous rate of time, a cuffles blood pressure monitoring system is introduced that works by obtaining a PPG signal from the body. The main purpose of this study is to develop a cuffless blood pressure monitoring system that uses Photoplethysmography (PPG) signals to circumvent these limitations. This method prioritizes user convenience and accessibility while ensuring precise blood pressure evaluation by extracting important features. This research focuses on both the development of the system and the extensive use of MATLAB and experimental analysis in different age groups. This innovative technology represents a huge leap forward in the field of cardiovascular health monitoring, emphasizing precision and ease of use. This system has the potential to improve healthcare accessibility by reducing reliance on specialist medical knowledge and eliminating the need for per-person calibration during use, as blood pressure is estimated using a population-trained regression model derived from cuff-based reference measurements.

title: Deep Learning-Enhanced Cloud Accounting Model for Real-Time Fraud and Financial Risk Prediction
authors: Saiprakash Kodela, Sai Bhuvana Kurada, Venkata Babu Mogili, Jagadeesh Duggirala
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Digital financial transactions have enhanced the chances of fraud and financial mismanagement, thus the need to have a strong real-time predictive system. Fraud detection and financial risk prediction are a major problem due to the multidimensional and intricate patterns of transactions. This paper introduces a deep learning boosted cloud accounting model, which can overcome these challenges that uses ResNet+UNet to extract hierarchical features, the Enhanced Doctor and Patient Optimization (EDPO) algorithm to select the top features, as well as the Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) to predict fraud and financial risk in real-time. The model is tested on Kaggle synthetic financial dataset which includes six million transactions that represent various and realistic frauds. The findings indicate that the proposed work reaches an accuracy of 99.71%, which is greater than the traditional machine-learning algorithms, including the Random Forest (98.62%) and XGBoost (99.15%). The Brier Score is 0.0045 and log loss is 0.178, reaching a high value of 0.984, which means increased reliability and calibration. Top-K analysis shows that the model can detect 976 out of 1,000 fraudulent transactions and thus it shows that a deep feature extraction, best selection, and reinforcement learning can enable the creation of an accurate and scalable cloud-based system to predict fraud and risk in real-time.

title: An Intelligent Crop Recommendation System Using Ensemble and Probabilistic Learning Approaches
authors: Adesh Kumar, Abhishek Mathur, Manish Kumar Mishra, Amandeep Kaur, Kamalpreet Kaur, Dipika Mal
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: India where agriculture is the primary livelihood for a large population, recommending the most suitable crop based on environmental and soil parameters can drastically improve yield and resource utilization. This project uses a data set of various soil and weather parameters to develop a machine learning model that recommends the ideal crop for a given set of conditions. The system leverages classification algorithms and evaluates their performance to select the most accurate predictor. Precision agriculture is emerging as a transformative approach to improve productivity and sustainability in modern agriculture. This project presents an intelligent crop recommendation system powered by supervised machine learning techniques. Using a curated dataset comprising 2200 records with agroclimatic parameters — namely nitrogen (N), phosphorus (P), potassium (K), temperature, humidity, pH, and rainfall — the system is designed to predict the most suitable crop for given environmental conditions. A comparative analysis of ten classification algorithms including Naive Bayes, Random Forest, and Support Vector Machine (SVM) was conducted. The Naive Bayes model demonstrated superior predictive performance, achieving a peak accuracy of 99.5%. The proposed framework offers a scalable decision-support mechanism for farmers, aligning with the objectives of precision agriculture.

title: Signal Processing in Communication Systems Using Machine Learning Approaches
authors: Shreesha Kalkoor Mudradi, Meenakshi Maindola, Smitha K, Nazeer Shaik, Harshal Patil, Natrayan L
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Signal control is considered to play a pivotal role in increasing the efficiency and dependability of the current communication networks. This analysis looks at how AI methods can be combined to address improve signal handling work processes, with a focus on three stages, namely, features selection, highlight determination and characterization. In signal information preprocessing Basic standardisation strategy is employed ensuring that the review is standardised and is working on the foundations of the results obtained. Throughout this paper, PCA is used to identify highlights and it is demonstrated that PCA can successfully reduce dimensions and maintain the fundamental data format whilst increasing the computational efficiency. The characterization is done using Angle Helping Machines (GBMs) because it is capable of processing the Information Appropriation and it is very accurate. Experimental results testify that it processes huge improvements on signal order quality in addition to speed contrast to traditional methods. Capable of addressing the problems of correspondence structures, the proposed framework suggests technology capable of accommodating the real implementation to an accommodating and effective degree. The analysis presented in this work paves the way even further to other developments in adroit sign management.

title: A Bibliometric Analysis of Victim Identification through AI-Based Image Forensics
authors: Smita Khairnar, Shilpa Gite, Keya Jadhav, Mohini Jadhav, Om Jadhav, Atharv Jadhav
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Advancements in Artificial Intelligence (AI) have significantly influenced digital image-based image forensic science by using super-advanced techniques to identify victims from visual evidence that is degraded, tampered, or manipulated. The fast spread of deepfakes, spoofing attacks, and synthetic media has raised serious doubts about the reliability of digital evidence; thus, traditional matcher-based techniques, manual visual inspection, and handcrafted feature descriptors are becoming less and less effective. As a result, research on AI-powered image restoration, recognition, and verification methods has increased dramatically, with the aim of recovering trustworthy visual cues from the degraded data. This research carries out an exhaustive bibliometric study of AI-based image forensics for victim identifi-cation, with Scopus as the main data source and Web of Science and Google Scholar for cross-verification. Structured Boolean queries led to the retrieval of 186 peer-reviewed documents published between 2015 and 2025, which were then analyzed with VOSviewer (version 1.6.19). The analysis is an examination of publication growth trends, citation patterns, co-authorship networks, keyword co-occurrences, top authors, institutions, source titles, and country contributions. The quantitative results show an almost nine-fold increase in annual publications, and the peak year is 2023 (54 documents), which represent the rapid adoption of deep learning methods in forensic investigation studies. The result of keyword co-occurrence analysis indicates a strong convergence of computer vision with digital forensics studies, therefore the most thematic clusters are centered on face recognition, deep learning-based image restoration, and anti-spoofing. Lastly, geographical analysis reveals China as the most prominent contributor, followed by substantial contributions from the United Kingdom and Saudi Arabia, while institutional collaboration networks demonstrate an increasing global and interdisciplinary approach.In conclusion, the research shows that AI-based image forensics has transformed into a quickly growing research domain, characterized by increasing methodological maturity, collaborative research arrangements, and forensic relevance with digital image forensics, computer vision, and law enforcement technologies [1].

title: A Static Sign Language Interpretation for Individuals with Hearing Impairment based on Deep CNN Framework
authors: Sanjoy Debnath, Amit Baran Dey, Ravi Singh Kurmvanshi, Ritwik Haldar, Sunandita Debnath, Wasim Arif
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: A highly efficient gesture recognition system is necessary for hearing-impaired persons to express their thoughts. An effective model that translate sign language to words is valuable for such communication. In this paper, we proposed a deep convolutional neural net-work (CNN) based approach to recognize the hand gesture accurately. American Sign Language (ASL) that consist of around 87,000 images of 26 classes (A-Z) is used for training purpose of the model. A comparative analysis is performed with the VGG-19 and AlexNet. The proposed model shows a significant performance characteristics with an accuracy of 98.70% as compare to the state-of-the-art models. The achieved results demonstrates the possible applicability of the proposed model in recognition of real-time hand gestures. The achieved results also highlights the potential incorporation of the proposed model in assistive and interactive communication systems.

title: AI-Driven Impact Analysis of Clinical Parameters Influencing Menopause
authors: Mane Krushna Balaji, Kiruthika V, Meghavika Baidya, Shery Angel Rajakumar, Kulkarni Atharva Gopinath
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Menopause, a critical transition in a woman's life, is associated with diverse symptoms. Most existing studies focus on the analysis of menopause severity based on a single or specific factor only. Interrelationship study between the influencing factors and severity of menopause remains under explored. This study explores the impact of various clinical parameters on menopause severity using a data-driven approach to improve clinical understanding, interpretability and management. By analyzing key clinical parametersincluding symptoms, hormonal levels, diagnostic, demographic parameters-this research identifies the most contributing features that influence the severity of menopause. The workflow involves data preprocessing, feature selection and validation. Initially the data is cleaned, and data imbalance is addressed using Synthetic Minority Over-sampling Technique. To facilitate the identification of key factors, feature selection techniques are integrated with machine learning techniques like Support Vector Machines (SVM), K-Nearest Neighbors (KNN), Random Forest, and XGBoost. Model evaluation and hyperparameter tuning help to make reliable identification of the impactful features. The identified clinical features includeSystolic Blood Pressure, Estrogen, Free Triiodothyronine, Triglycerides, Free Thyroxine, Progesterone, Age, Follicle Stimulating Hormone, Prolactin and Body Mass Index whose significance was validated through statistical analyses and clinical evidence. This approach serves as a clinical decision support system aiding tailored treatment and management of menopause severity.

title: Menstrual Health Monitoring and Prediction System Using Machine Learning
authors: Janvi Gulati, Dhanashree Chila, Sneha Bhamare, Swati Nadkarni, Minakshi Retharekar, Sneha Chila
publish: 2026 Twelfth International Conference on Bio Signals, Images, and Instrumentation (ICBSII)
abstract: Women's reproductive health depends on tracking their menstrual health, but the majority of tracking apps are calendar-based and lack predictive intelligence. In this paper, we introduce DateIncoming, a machine learning-based intelligent menstrual health tracking system that offers health risk analysis, symptom forecasts, and personalized predictions. Three domain-specific models are combined in the system: a weighted statistical approach with 86% accuracy for predicting cycles, a multi-output symptom prediction model with 82% accuracy for pain, flow, mood, and energy symptoms, and a condition risk indication model with 73% accuracy for various menstrual health conditions. The system is built with a secure backend, an interactive user interface, and Google Calendar integration. The app offers personalized recommendations, menstrual anomaly alerts, and next-day symptom forecasts.

title: Design and Performance Assessment of a PEM Fuel Cell–Supercapacitor Hybrid Powertrain for Green Electric Vehicles
authors: Vinaya B Koradoor, Rajath Shankar P. S, S Srinivasan, Jayachitra G, Janardhan L K
publish: 2026 Innovations in Machine, Engineering, and Digital Conference (IMED)
abstract: Rising demand for eco-friendly transport fuels the rise of green hydrogen electric vehicles (GHEVs). This study introduces a scalable prototype GHEV system combining PEM fuel cells (using Nafion 117 membranes with 25 cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> active area each in a two-cell series configuration), an efficient DC-DC boost converter, and supercapacitor-based hybrid energy storage. It covers the full hydrogen lifecycle from generation through vehicle integration, focusing on practical load balancing, voltage control, and system scalability factors such as weight and operating conditions. Tests demonstrated maximum PEM fuel cell power density of 0.32 W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> at 80°C and 3 atm pressure (ranging 0.18-0.32 W/cm<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">2</sup> for 60-80°C and 1-3 atm), yielding 55% efficiency, while the complete prototype delivered 40-55% efficiency during variable load cycles. Arduino Nano-based control ensures reliable power distribution between sources. Findings validate potential for commercial stack scaling and advance India’s sustainable hydrogen transport initiatives.

title: Resource Allocation Strategy for Multiple Maneuvering Target Tracking in Networked Radar System
authors: Jieyu Huang, Junwei Xie, Haowei Zhang, Weike Feng, Kun Wang
abstract: Networked collocated multiple-input multiple-output (C-MIMO) radar system has attracted more and more attention in recent years. Networked radar system can offer a higher tracking accuracy for multiple maneuvering targets tracking (MTT). This article proposes a joint resource allocation (JRA) strategy in networked C-MIMO radar system. Specifically, the power, bandwidth and beam resources for each radar are optimized by the JRA strategy to improve the MTT accuracy. The predicted conditional Cramér–Rao lower bound is utilized as the tracking evaluation criterion. A fast three-stage solution algorithm is proposed to solve the nonconvex and mixed-integer problem. The simulation results show that the strategy is effective and efficient.

title: Microwave Probe Based on an Inverted Microstrip Line for Broadband Electron Paramagnetic Resonance Spectroscopy
authors: Selina Eckel, Matthias Oliver Beck, Ahmet Çağrı Ulusoy
publish: IEEE Microwave and Wireless Technology Letters
abstract: This work presents a broadband inverted microstrip line (IMSL) as a microwave probe for electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) spectroscopy, a technique used to detect unpaired electron spins in biological and chemical samples. The IMSL geometry enables sample placement in the most homogeneous region of the microwave magnetic field, overcoming limitations of standard coplanar waveguide (CPW) and microstrip line (MSL) probes. Simulated and measured S-parameters confirm broadband operation up to 67 GHz. Improved field homogeneity relative to MSLs is demonstrated by a reduced coefficient of variation of the simulated magnetic field and by EPR measurements showing signal amplitudes independent of sample orientation. The IMSL geometry offers a compact microwave probe with straightforward fabrication for broadband EPR sensing in biological and healthcare applications.

title: AI-based Task Reallocation and Employee Productivity System
authors: K. Manoj, S. Manoj, N. Sathiyapriya
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Success of an organization depends on efficient management of tasks and productivity of the employees. Manual systems of task allocation tend to create uneven workload distribution, communication and less visibility of the work performance of the employees thus lowering the overall efficiency. The paper will describe an AI-Based Task Reallocation and Employee Productivity System, which is an automated system of task allocation depending on employee skills, existing workload, and availability. The system has provided continuity of workflow where tasks are dynamically re-allocated when there is an absence or leave so that operational disruption is minimal. A gamification module with adaptability incorporating points, badges, and leaderboards is adopted to increase levels of engagement with employees, motivation, and completion of tasks among employees. Real-time notification and alerts help to ensure a seamless flow of staff-management communication, and an AI-based analytics module allows taking actionable information on the workload trends, task completion rates, and performance indicators. The suggested system facilitates equity, transparency, and long-lasting productivity through the integration of smart assignment, dynamically assigned reassignments, gamification, and analytics. The solution provides organizations with the scalable, data-driven solution to workforce management, which tends to optimize the use of resources, enhance employee motivation, and facilitate professional development.

title: AI-based Video Manipulation Detection using Deep Learning
authors: K Mythili, Vimal Shankar A R, Sukhesh Kumar V, Sachin Vipu M D
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The rapid advancement of artificial intelligence has enabled the creation of highly realistic manipulated videos, commonly known as deepfakes. While these technologies offer creative potential, they also pose serious threats in the form of misinformation, identity fraud, and digital media manipulation. This paper presents an AI-based video manipulation detection system using deep learning techniques to identify forged and tampered videos. The proposed framework analyzes spatial inconsistencies in video frames using a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN). The system is trained and evaluated on the Face-Forensics++ and DeepFake Detection Challenge (DFDC) datasets comprising 3,000 videos. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method achieves an accuracy of 93.8%, precision of 94.1%, recall of 93.5%, and F1-score of 93.8%, with an inference speed of 35 FPS. These results indicate that spatial feature learning alone can provide reliable performance without relying on complex temporal modeling, making the system suitable for real-time applications such as social media monitoring, digital forensics, and cybersecurity.

title: TGAT-MARL: Deep Learning based Proactive Link Failure Prediction and Routing in MANETs
authors: D Kavitha, K Raghava Rao
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Mobile Ad Hoc Networks (MANETs) are highly vulnerable to frequent link failures due to node mobility, dynamic topology, and unstable wireless channels, which significantly degrade routing reliability and network performance. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a novel Proactive Link Failure Prediction and Reliable Routing framework based on Deep Learning-enabled Temporal Graph Attention and Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning (TGATMARL). The proposed approach leverages temporal graph attention networks to learn spatio-temporal link dynamics from realistic MANET mobility traces and proactively predicts imminent link failures, while a multi-agent reinforcement learning policy jointly optimizes routing decisions to ensure reliability and adaptability. The framework is implemented and evaluated using the SimPy simulator under a realistic MANET workload dataset derived from standard mobility and traffic patterns. Comprehensive experimental results demonstrate that the proposed TGAT-MARL approach significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods, including Multi-Agent Deep Learning (MADL), GAT-based PPO, and conventional DRL. Specifically, it achieves up to 14-22% improvement in packet delivery ratio, 18-27% reduction in end-to-end delay, 20-30% reduction in routing overhead, and 16-25% improvement in throughput. These gains confirm that proactive link failure awareness combined with cooperative multi-agent learning enables more robust, efficient, and scalable routing in highly dynamic MANET environments.

title: ResolveX: An AI-Driven Multilingual Grievance Management Framework using Transformer-based NLP
authors: Karthik K, Sakthi T S, Sivabalaji T, Sivasankar M
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: The recent advances in technology have enabled many companies to have better ways to deal with customer complaints than in the past. Today, most complaint management systems use out-of-date methods or only partially automated systems that lead to delayed responses, misaligned cases, and misdirected complaints. To solve this issue, we have developed an innovative complaint management system based on innovative language processing capabilities that enable customers to submit written complaints in multiple languages and receive a response with the correct department using an intelligent language processing algorithm that uses deep learning models that are based on the way humans read written text. In addition, we use another technological approach to measure the significance of each individual word in order to assess the urgency of a specific complaint. Additionally, the system provides customers with real-time notifications regarding the status of their complaints, maintains secure access to the customer's complaint data, provides graphical representations of the complaint's trends, and promotes effective resolution of complaints while instilling confidence in customers.

title: Behavior-Aware Insider Threat Detection in Cloud Systems using a Zero- Trust LSTM Model with Fuzzy Trust Assessment
authors: Eswari Petchiammal G, Abishek K S, Karthikeyan P, Agash Balaji B, Harishankar K
publish: 2026 6th International Conference on Expert Clouds and Applications (ICOECA)
abstract: Cloud infrastructures are increasingly at risk from inside attacks, in which authorized persons purposefully or intentionally compromise vital resources. Because they rely on static regulations, conventional access control systems are ineffective against dynamic behavioral aberrations. This study proposes Federated Zero Trust Behavioral Access Filtering (FZT-BAF), a novel architecture that combines Zero Trust principles for cross-cloud security with federated behavioral machine learning to address this issue. Unlike centralized models, the proposed system guarantees strong privacy preservation by allowing several cloud nodes to collaboratively train behavioral anomaly models without exchanging raw user data. To rain a local anomaly detection model, each node locally computers user behavior embeddings from login patterns, resource consumption, command sequences, file access behavior, and temporal data. These updates are integrated into a global behavioral risk model using a secure Federated Gradient Sharing method. To determine adaptive MFA challenges, access approvals, or constraints, the Zero Trust Access Engine imposes the global model continually estimate each user's Dynamic Trust Score (DTS). Experimental assessment utilizing simulated cross-cloud behavioral datasets suggests that FZT-BAF delivers better detection accuracy for insider anomalies while reducing data exposure by 100%. Based on the results, the federated model improves non-federated baselines in anomaly detection through 8-15%.
